## 1. Subsystem Purpose and Boundaries

The `k8s.io/apiserver` library (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/`) is a reusable framework for building production-grade, Kubernetes-style API servers. It is the foundation for `kube-apiserver` itself and for extension API servers written by third parties (aggregated API servers, CRD-backed servers, etc.).

**What it does:**
- Provides the `GenericAPIServer` runtime: HTTP serving, TLS, lifecycle management, health endpoints.
- Implements the full request pipeline: authentication → authorization → priority-and-fairness → audit → admission → REST dispatch → storage.
- Offers pluggable backends for every stage of that pipeline via well-defined Go interfaces.
- Bridges between the HTTP world and the typed Go world via serialization, conversion, and versioning machinery sourced from `k8s.io/apimachinery`.

**What it does not do:**
- Define concrete API types (those live in `k8s.io/api` and in individual API group packages).
- Run controllers or reconciliation loops (that is `controller-manager` territory).
- Talk to clients other than etcd (outbound calls to webhooks and extension servers go through `k8s.io/client-go`).

**Upstream / downstream dependencies:**

| Direction | Dependency | Role |
|-----------|-----------|------|
| Upstream | `k8s.io/apimachinery` | Scheme, runtime.Object, serialization codecs, GVK primitives |
| Upstream | `k8s.io/client-go` | REST client, informer factory (used by admission webhooks and PAF) |
| Upstream | `etcd` (via `go.etcd.io/etcd/client/v3`) | Primary and only supported backing store |
| Downstream | `kube-apiserver` (`cmd/kube-apiserver`) | Main consumer; calls `CreateServerChain` |
| Downstream | `k8s.io/kube-aggregator` | Aggregation layer; embeds `GenericAPIServer` |
| Downstream | `k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver` | CRD server; embeds `GenericAPIServer` |
| Downstream | Any aggregated API server | Extension servers built using `RecommendedOptions` |

---

## 2. Key Components and Responsibility Split

### 2.1 `pkg/server` — The Core Runtime

**`Config` struct** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go:112`)
The central configuration object for `GenericAPIServer`. It is populated from command-line flags via `Options` structs and passed to `New()`. Fields include `Authentication`, `Authorization`, `AdmissionControl`, `FlowControl`, `AuditBackend`, `BuildHandlerChainFunc`, and `SecureServing`. Notably, it exposes `BuildHandlerChainFunc` as a first-class escape hatch for replacing the entire filter chain.

**`GenericAPIServer` struct** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go:110`)
The server instance. It holds the `APIServerHandler` (which carries the live `http.Handler`), its delegation target, lifecycle signals, post-start hooks, pre-shutdown hooks, health registries, and the audit backend. The type does not export a `Start()` method directly; callers must first call `PrepareRun()` which returns the opaque `preparedGenericAPIServer`, and then call `RunWithContext()` on that.

**`APIServerHandler` struct** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/handler.go:42`)
Holds three layered handlers:
- `FullHandlerChain` — the entire filter-decorated handler. `ServeHTTP` on this struct dispatches here.
- `GoRestfulContainer` — the `go-restful` WebService container used for registered API groups.
- `NonGoRestfulMux` — a `PathRecorderMux` for everything else (healthz, metrics, openapi, debug).
- `Director` — chooses between the two above based on registered path prefixes.

**`DelegationTarget` interface** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go:312`)
The delegation chain contract. Each server in the chain implements this; unhandled requests fall through to `NextDelegate()`. `emptyDelegate` terminates the chain, returning 404.

**`lifecycleSignals`** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/lifecycle_signals.go:112`)
Named channels signaling state transitions: `ShutdownInitiated`, `AfterShutdownDelayDuration`, `PreShutdownHooksStopped`, `NotAcceptingNewRequest`, `InFlightRequestsDrained`, `HTTPServerStoppedListening`, `HasBeenReady`, `MuxAndDiscoveryComplete`. Filters in the handler chain subscribe to these channels to drain requests gracefully during shutdown.

### 2.2 `pkg/endpoints` — HTTP-to-REST Bridge

**`APIInstaller`** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/installer.go:62`)
Introspects a `rest.Storage` implementation at server startup and registers the appropriate go-restful routes for each supported verb (GET, LIST, WATCH, CREATE, UPDATE, PATCH, DELETE, and subresources). Routes are annotated with `x-kubernetes-action` and `x-kubernetes-group-version-kind` metadata used by OpenAPI generation.

**Verb handlers** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/`)
Concrete handlers for each HTTP method: `create.go`, `update.go`, `delete.go`, `get.go`, `patch.go`, `watch.go`. Each reads the request body, negotiates content type, calls into the admission chain, and dispatches to the underlying `rest.Storage`. For example, `createHandler` (`handlers/create.go:53`) deserializes the body only after the filter chain has authenticated and authorized the request.

**`RequestScope`** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/`)
A per-route context snapshot containing the serializer, kind, namer, and admission interface — passed to every verb handler at dispatch time, eliminating repeated lookups.

### 2.3 `pkg/admission` — Policy Enforcement

**`Interface` / `MutationInterface` / `ValidationInterface`** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/interfaces.go:122`)
The admission plugin contracts. `MutationInterface.Admit` may modify the object; `ValidationInterface.Validate` may only reject it. Both receive `Attributes` (operation, object, old object, user info) and `ObjectInterfaces` (convertor, defaulter).

**`chainAdmissionHandler`** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/chain.go:23`)
A `[]Interface` that implements both mutation and validation by iterating plugins in order and short-circuiting on the first error.

**`reinvoker`** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/reinvocation.go`)
Wraps the mutation chain to re-run mutating plugins after any individual plugin sets `ShouldReinvoke()` on the `ReinvocationContext`. This supports webhook plugins whose mutations may invalidate earlier plugins' assumptions.

**Built-in admission plugins** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/`)
The library ships with: `namespace/lifecycle`, `resourcequota`, `webhook/mutating`, `webhook/validating`, `policy/mutating` (MutatingAdmissionPolicy, alpha), `policy/validating` (ValidatingAdmissionPolicy, GA).

### 2.4 `pkg/storage` — Storage Abstraction

**`storage.Interface`** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/interfaces.go`)
The storage contract defining `Create`, `Get`, `GuaranteedUpdate`, `Delete`, `Watch`, and `List`. The only production implementation today is `etcd3`.

**`Cacher`** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/cacher.go:262`)
An in-memory write-through layer placed in front of the raw etcd client. It maintains a `watchCache` (a sliding-window event ring buffer) and a `cache.Reflector`. WATCH and LIST requests are primarily served from memory; only consistent reads must wait for (or fetch directly from) etcd.

**`genericregistry.Store`** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic/registry/store.go:100`)
The standard CRUD implementation used by built-in and extension resources. It wraps `storage.Interface` in a `DryRunnableStorage` wrapper (to handle dry-run requests) and calls into resource-specific `Strategy` objects for validation, defaulting, and conflict detection.

### 2.5 `pkg/util/flowcontrol` — Priority and Fairness

**`apf_controller`** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/apf_controller.go`)
A controller that watches `FlowSchema` and `PriorityLevelConfiguration` objects and configures a set of `QueueSet` fair queues. When a request arrives, the controller matches it against `FlowSchema` rules (by user, group, verb, resource, namespace) and assigns it to a priority level.

**`QueueSet` / fair queuing** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/fairqueuing/queueset/`)
Implements shuffle-sharding fair queuing. Each priority level has multiple queues. A request's queue is selected by hashing its flow distinguisher (typically `<username>/<namespace>`) against the queue count (shuffle sharding). Work is measured in seat-seconds via a `WorkEstimator`; list requests with many objects cost more "seats" than simple GETs.

### 2.6 `pkg/audit` — Audit Logging

**`Sink` / `Backend`** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit/types.go:22`)
`Sink.ProcessEvents` receives audit events at each stage of a request. `Backend` extends `Sink` with `Run/Shutdown` lifecycle. Multiple backends may be unioned with `audit.Union`.

**`PolicyRuleEvaluator`** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit/evaluator.go:40`)
Evaluates the audit policy file against authorizer attributes and returns a `RequestAuditConfig` with a `Level` (None/Metadata/Request/RequestResponse) and stages to omit.

---

## 3. End-to-End Request Flow

The following describes what happens from the time an HTTP request arrives until a response is sent. File citations reference the actual construction order in `DefaultBuildHandlerChain` (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go:1028`).

> **Key insight**: `DefaultBuildHandlerChain` builds the chain from the _inside out_ — the innermost handler (closest to storage) is wrapped by successively outer filters. The code reads bottom-to-top in the source; the first handler to receive a request is the outermost one (last applied in the source).

### 3.1 Filter Chain (outermost to innermost)

```
Client
  │
  ▼
[1] WithAuditInit               — allocates an AuditContext into the request context
[2] WithPanicRecovery           — recovers from panics; logs stack trace; returns 500
[3] WithMuxAndDiscoveryComplete — returns 503 if the server has not finished installing paths
[4] WithRequestReceivedTimestamp— stamps arrival time into context
[5] WithRequestInfo             — parses URL into RequestInfo (verb, resource, namespace, name)
[6] WithRoutine (optional)      — offloads request to a separate goroutine (APIServingWithRoutine gate)
[7] WithHTTPLogging             — structured HTTP access log
[8] WithRetryAfter (optional)   — during shutdown, 429 + Retry-After header
[9] WithHSTS                    — adds Strict-Transport-Security header
[10] WithCacheControl           — sets Cache-Control: no-cache
[11] WithProbabilisticGoaway    — randomly sends HTTP/2 GOAWAY to load-balance long-running connections
[12] WithWatchTermination...    — drains watch requests during shutdown
[13] WithWaitGroup              — adds non-long-running requests to a SafeWaitGroup for graceful drain
[14] WithRequestDeadline        — enforces overall request deadline from context
[15] WithTimeoutForNonLong...   — runs handler in a goroutine with a deadline; returns 504 on timeout
[16] WithWarningRecorder        — installs a warning header writer (thread-safe under the timeout wrapper)
[17] WithCORS                   — preflight and Access-Control-* headers
[18] WithAuthentication         — calls Authenticator chain; injects UserInfo into context; 401 on failure
[19] WithTracing (optional)     — creates OpenTelemetry span from authenticated context
[20] WithAudit                  — emits audit events at RequestReceived and ResponseComplete stages
[21] WithImpersonation          — applies impersonation headers (if Impersonate-* headers are present)
[22] WithPriorityAndFairness /  — classifies request into a FlowSchema/PriorityLevel; queues or rejects
    WithMaxInFlightLimit
[23] WithAuthorization          — calls Authorizer chain; 403 on denial
[24] (API handler / REST dispatch)
      │
      ▼
    Director → GoRestfulContainer → APIInstaller route
                                        │
                                        ▼
                                  Verb handler (e.g. createHandler)
                                        │
                                        ├── Deserialize request body
                                        ├── Default object fields
                                        ├── Admission: Mutate (Admit)
                                        ├── Validate (Strategy + admission Validate)
                                        └── genericregistry.Store.Create/Update/Delete
                                                │
                                                ▼
                                          DryRunnableStorage
                                                │
                                                ▼
                                          Cacher / etcd3
```

### 3.2 A Concrete CREATE Example

1. **Request arrives**: `POST /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/default/deployments`
2. `WithRequestInfo` parses this as `{verb:create, resource:deployments, group:apps, version:v1, namespace:default}`.
3. `WithAuthentication` validates the bearer token against the OIDC/x509 authenticator chain. UserInfo is injected.
4. `WithPriorityAndFairness` matches the request to a `FlowSchema` (e.g. `system:authenticated`), places it in a queue for the `workload-high` priority level. The `WorkEstimator` assigns 1 seat (simple mutating write).
5. `WithAuthorization` calls RBAC — checks if the user has `create` on `deployments` in `default` namespace.
6. `WithAudit` emits a `RequestReceived` event.
7. The go-restful `Director` identifies the path is registered under `/apis/apps/v1` and dispatches to the registered go-restful route.
8. `createHandler` (`handlers/create.go:53`) is invoked:
   - Content negotiation for request and response encodings.
   - `limitedReadBodyWithRecordMetric` reads the body (up to `MaxRequestBodyBytes`).
   - Decoder deserializes body into internal type (`apps/v1` → internal `apps`).
   - Defaulting functions set optional fields.
   - Admission `Admit` phase: mutating webhook plugins (and `MutatingAdmissionPolicy`) are called.
   - Admission `Validate` phase: validating admission plugins (Strategy + `ValidatingAdmissionWebhook` + `ValidatingAdmissionPolicy`) are called.
   - `genericregistry.Store.Create` is invoked → `DryRunnableStorage.Create` → `Cacher.Create` → `etcd3.Create`.
   - `WithAudit` emits `ResponseComplete` with the response object (if audit level ≥ `RequestResponse`).
9. HTTP 201 Created is written back to the client.

---

## 4. Server Composition and Delegation Chain

`kube-apiserver` is not a single server; it is a chain of three `GenericAPIServer` instances assembled in `cmd/kube-apiserver/app/server.go:CreateServerChain`.

```
Incoming request
      │
      ▼
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│  Aggregator Server          │  (kube-aggregator)
│  Handles: APIService CRD    │
│  Proxies: extension servers │
└──────────┬──────────────────┘
           │ delegate (not an APIService path)
           ▼
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│  Kube API Server (core)     │
│  Handles: /api/v1, /apis/*  │
│  built-in groups             │
└──────────┬──────────────────┘
           │ delegate (unknown path)
           ▼
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│  API Extensions Server      │  (apiextensions-apiserver)
│  Handles: CRDs dynamically  │
└──────────┬──────────────────┘
           │ delegate (end of chain)
           ▼
      emptyDelegate → 404
```

**How delegation works**: Each `GenericAPIServer` stores a `delegationTarget DelegationTarget`. When the `Director`'s `NonGoRestfulMux` cannot match a path, it calls the `UnprotectedHandler()` of the delegation target — effectively bypassing the *target's* filter chain (authentication, authorization, etc. run only once, in the outermost server). This is why `UnprotectedHandler()` returns the target's `Handler.Director` directly, not its `FullHandlerChain`.

**Post-start and pre-shutdown hooks** are aggregated across the chain: each server's hooks are merged with its delegation target's hooks so that a single `RunWithContext` drives the entire system.

---

## 5. API Group Registration

### 5.1 The `runtime.Scheme`

The `runtime.Scheme` is the type registry for a group. It provides:
- **GVK ↔ Go type mapping**: registers types so the serialization framework can create the right Go struct from a JSON/protobuf payload.
- **Conversion functions**: the hub-and-spoke model converts all versions through a single internal type (the "hub"). `conversion-gen` generates most conversion functions; handwritten conversions handle non-trivial field renames.
- **Defaulting functions**: `defaulter-gen` generates `SetDefaults_*` functions that are registered into the scheme and called during decode.
- **Deep-copy functions**: `deepcopy-gen` generates `DeepCopyObject()` required by `runtime.Object`. This is safety-critical: objects returned from the watch cache must be deep-copied before modification to prevent data races.

### 5.2 `APIGroupInfo` and Registration

```go
// staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go:72
type APIGroupInfo struct {
    PrioritizedVersions         []schema.GroupVersion
    VersionedResourcesStorageMap map[string]map[string]rest.Storage
    Scheme                      *runtime.Scheme
    NegotiatedSerializer        runtime.NegotiatedSerializer
    ParameterCodec              runtime.ParameterCodec
}
```

`VersionedResourcesStorageMap` maps `version → resource-name → rest.Storage`. Each resource can have multiple sub-resources (e.g., `"deployments/status"`, `"deployments/scale"`), each with its own `rest.Storage` implementation.

`GenericAPIServer.InstallAPIGroups` calls `APIInstaller.Install()` which:
1. Iterates resources in `VersionedResourcesStorageMap`.
2. For each resource, introspects which `rest.*` interfaces its `Storage` implements (`rest.Getter`, `rest.Lister`, `rest.Creater`, `rest.Updater`, `rest.GracefulDeleter`, `rest.Watcher`, etc.) via type assertion.
3. Registers go-restful routes for only the verbs supported by that storage.
4. Populates discovery documents and OpenAPI schema at the same time.

### 5.3 The `Strategy` Pattern

Each resource's `genericregistry.Store` is configured with up to three strategy objects:

```go
// staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic/registry/store.go:172
CreateStrategy rest.RESTCreateStrategy
UpdateStrategy rest.RESTUpdateStrategy
DeleteStrategy rest.RESTDeleteStrategy
```

A strategy object implements the resource-specific validation and business logic that is shared across all storage backends. For example, `Deployment`'s strategy validates pod template specs, resets fields that users may not set (like `status`), and enforces name generation rules. This separation keeps the generic storage machinery clean and resource-specific logic co-located with its type definition.

---

## 6. Storage Layer and Watch Cache

### 6.1 Architecture

```
REST handler
    │
    ▼
genericregistry.Store
    │  (wraps in DryRunnableStorage for dry-run)
    ▼
Cacher  (staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/cacher.go:262)
    │   ┌──────────────────────────┐
    │   │  watchCache              │  ← ring buffer of recent events
    │   │  cache.Reflector         │  ← LIST + WATCH from etcd
    │   └──────────────────────────┘
    │  (falls through for consistent reads or cache misses)
    ▼
etcd3.Store (grpc to etcd cluster)
```

### 6.2 Cacher Internals

The `Cacher` struct (`cacher.go:262`) maintains:
- `watchCache`: a fixed-capacity ring buffer of `watchCacheEvent` objects, protected by an `RWMutex`. The buffer size defaults to 100 (`EtcdOptions.DefaultWatchCacheSize`), configurable per-resource via `--watch-cache-sizes`.
- `reflector`: a `cache.Reflector` that issues a single LIST + ongoing WATCH against etcd for the resource prefix. Events are pushed into `incoming chan watchCacheEvent` and then dispatched by the cacher's main goroutine to registered `cacheWatcher` clients.
- `watchers`: an `indexedWatchers` map that organizes active client watches by namespace and by an optional indexed trigger (e.g., for pod watches filtered by `spec.nodeName`). The trigger optimization avoids waking every watcher on every event.

### 6.3 Serving Semantics

| Request type | Served from |
|---|---|
| `?watch=true&resourceVersion=0` | In-memory ring buffer from the start |
| `?watch=true&resourceVersion=<rv>` | In-memory buffer if rv is within window; otherwise etcd watch |
| `LIST` (no RV, or `resourceVersion=0`) | Watch cache store snapshot |
| Consistent `LIST` (`resourceVersionMatch=NotOlderThan` with progress notify) | Waits for cache to advance past etcd's latest rev; served from cache |
| `LIST` with exact `resourceVersion` and a limit | Falls through to etcd (pagination not fully cached) |

Bookmarks (empty `WATCH` events carrying only a `resourceVersion`) prevent the cache's RV from becoming stale — a stale cache would force expensive relist operations on clients.

### 6.4 Optimistic Concurrency

Every object stored in etcd carries a `resourceVersion` that maps 1-to-1 to etcd's global `mod_revision`. On `UPDATE` or `PATCH`, `GuaranteedUpdate` performs a `txn` in etcd that only commits if the key's `mod_revision` matches the expected value. A mismatch returns `409 Conflict`, requiring the client to re-read and resubmit. This is pure optimistic locking — no server-side distributed locks are held during request processing.

---

## 7. Extension Points and Customization Hooks

### 7.1 Custom Handler Chain (`BuildHandlerChainFunc`)

```go
// staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go:186
BuildHandlerChainFunc func(apiHandler http.Handler, c *Config) (secure http.Handler)
```

By replacing `Config.BuildHandlerChainFunc`, a consumer can insert, remove, or reorder any filter in the chain. The library ships two implementations:
- `DefaultBuildHandlerChain` — the standard production chain.
- `BuildHandlerChainWithStorageVersionPrecondition` — adds `WithStorageVersionPrecondition` (used by `kube-apiserver`) to gate requests until storage version migration is complete.

**Risk**: Replacing the handler chain can silently disable authentication or authorization. Any custom chain should call `DefaultBuildHandlerChain` and wrap its result, rather than starting from scratch.

### 7.2 Post-Start and Pre-Shutdown Hooks

```go
// GenericAPIServer method
AddPostStartHook(name string, hook PostStartHookFunc) error
AddPreShutdownHook(name string, hook preShutdownHookFunc) error
```

Post-start hooks run after the server starts listening, in separate goroutines. They receive a `StopCh` channel that closes when shutdown is initiated. Pre-shutdown hooks run before the HTTP server stops accepting connections — they are appropriate for draining outbound connections or flipping health check states.

**Risk**: Slow or blocking post-start hooks delay the server's `HasBeenReady` signal, causing the process to be killed by liveness probes in managed environments.

### 7.3 Admission Plugins

Admission plugins are the primary policy enforcement extension point. A custom in-process plugin must:

1. Implement `admission.Interface` (and optionally `MutationInterface` or `ValidationInterface`).
2. Register itself: `plugins.Register(PluginName, factory)` — where `plugins` is the `Plugins` registry passed into `AdmissionOptions`.
3. If it needs shared resources (e.g., an informer factory or a Kubernetes client), implement the appropriate `WantsX` interface from `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/initializer/interfaces.go`:
   - `WantsExternalKubeInformerFactory`
   - `WantsAuthorizer`
   - `WantsRESTMapper`
   - `WantsFeatures`

For external validation without in-process code, `ValidatingAdmissionWebhook` (`plugin/webhook/validating`) and the CEL-based `ValidatingAdmissionPolicy` (`plugin/policy/validating`) ship with the library and require no code changes.

**Risk of re-invocation**: Mutating webhooks may be re-invoked if a sibling webhook set `ShouldReinvoke()`. Plugins must therefore be idempotent — applying the same mutation twice must have the same effect as applying it once.

### 7.4 Authenticator and Authorizer Composition

Authentication is composed via `authenticatorunion` (first-success wins). Authorizers are composed via a chain where each authorizer can return Allow, Deny, or NoOpinion; NoOpinion passes to the next. Adding a custom authenticator or authorizer requires modifying `Config.Authentication.Authenticator` or `Config.Authorization.Authorizer` before `New()` is called.

For extension servers, `DelegatingAuthenticationOptions` and `DelegatingAuthorizationOptions` (in `pkg/server/options/`) issue `SubjectAccessReview` requests to the main `kube-apiserver`, delegating all auth decisions — this is the standard pattern for aggregated API servers.

### 7.5 `RecommendedOptions` for Extension Servers

```go
// staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/options/recommended.go:34
type RecommendedOptions struct {
    Etcd           *EtcdOptions
    SecureServing  *SecureServingOptionsWithLoopback
    Authentication *DelegatingAuthenticationOptions
    Authorization  *DelegatingAuthorizationOptions
    Audit          *AuditOptions
    Features       *FeatureOptions
    CoreAPI        *CoreAPIOptions
    Admission      *AdmissionOptions
    EgressSelector *EgressSelectorOptions
    Traces         *TracingOptions
}
```

This struct is the standard entry point for building a new aggregated API server. Calling `ApplyTo(config)` applies each option group to the server config in dependency order (etcd → egress → TLS → auth → audit → admission). Extension server authors only need to set the fields that differ from defaults.

### 7.6 `genericregistry.Store` Hooks

The `Store` exposes three sets of lifecycle hooks per operation:

| Hook | Timing | Use case |
|------|--------|----------|
| `BeginCreate` / `BeginUpdate` | Before storage write; returns commit/revert | Acquire side-effect resources transactionally |
| `AfterCreate` / `AfterUpdate` / `AfterDelete` | After storage write, before decoration | Emit metrics, invalidate caches |
| `Decorator` | On every object returned from storage | Inject computed fields not stored in etcd |
| `ShouldDeleteDuringUpdate` | During update | Implement finalizer-style soft-delete |

**Risk**: `BeginCreate/BeginUpdate` revert functions must be idempotent because they may be called more than once if the framework retries internally.

---

## 8. Failure Modes, Limits, and Design Tradeoffs

### 8.1 etcd Dependency

The `apiserver` library has a hard dependency on a live etcd cluster. There is no in-memory fallback for production use. A full etcd partition causes write operations to fail immediately (etcd requires a quorum write). Read operations that hit the watch cache can continue to succeed for cached data, but consistent reads (`resourceVersionMatch=NotOlderThan`) will block until `waitUntilFreshAndBlock` times out.

### 8.2 Watch Cache Eviction

The watch cache ring buffer is bounded. When a slow client falls behind the write rate, its cached RV drops out of the buffer window and the watcher receives an `ERROR` event with `410 Gone`. The client must restart its watch from `resourceVersion=0`. This is a known operational cliff: high write rates or large ring buffer sizes (configured per-resource) directly trade memory for client resilience. The default buffer size is 100 events per resource type.

### 8.3 Priority and Fairness vs. MaxInFlight

The library supports two concurrency-control mechanisms:
- **`MaxInFlightLimit`** (`pkg/server/filters/maxinflight.go`): a simple counting semaphore with separate limits for mutating (`MaxMutatingRequestsInFlight`) and non-mutating (`MaxRequestsInFlight`) requests. Any request exceeding the limit gets an immediate `429 Too Many Requests`.
- **Priority and Fairness (APF)**: replaces `MaxInFlightLimit` when `Config.FlowControl` is non-nil. APF queues requests rather than immediately rejecting them, and enforces per-flow fairness using shuffle-sharded fair queuing.

APF is the default in `kube-apiserver` since 1.29. Extension servers using `RecommendedOptions` do not enable APF by default and fall back to `MaxInFlightLimit`. APF requires `FlowSchema` and `PriorityLevelConfiguration` objects in the API to exist before it can configure itself — creating a startup bootstrap dependency.

### 8.4 Admission Re-invocation Overhead

The `reinvoker` wrapper (`pkg/admission/reinvocation.go`) can run the mutating admission chain twice per write request. With many heavy mutating webhooks, this doubles the worst-case webhook call latency. Operators should use `reinvocationPolicy: IfNeeded` selectively on `MutatingWebhookConfiguration` objects rather than globally.

### 8.5 Handler Chain Construction Ordering

`DefaultBuildHandlerChain` is built inside-out (code reads bottom-to-top). Misplacing a filter — e.g., moving `WithRequestInfo` after `WithAuthentication` — silently breaks features that depend on `RequestInfo` being in context (such as RBAC authorization, which inspects resource/verb). The framework does not validate the chain ordering at startup; bugs manifest at runtime under specific request patterns.

### 8.6 Graceful Shutdown Sequencing

The shutdown flow (`RunWithContext`, `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go:536`) relies on a precise signal ordering:

```
SIGTERM/ctx.Done()
    → ShutdownInitiated (readyz → red)
    → PreShutdownHooks run concurrently
    → AfterShutdownDelayDuration (~70s default)
    → NotAcceptingNewRequest
        → NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup.Wait (up to ShutdownTimeout ~60s)
        → WatchRequestWaitGroup.Wait
    → InFlightRequestsDrained
        → HTTP server stops listening
        → AuditBackend.Shutdown()
```

A pre-shutdown hook that blocks longer than `ShutdownDelayDuration` can prevent the server from draining active requests cleanly. `WithWaitGroup` ensures that each non-long-running request adds itself to `NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup` only if it is not exempt from `RetryAfter` logic, so these two mechanisms must agree on what "long-running" means.

---

## 9. Operational Implications

### 9.1 Performance: Watch Cache Sizing

Each watched resource type maintains its own ring buffer. Resources with high write throughput (e.g., `Events`, `Pods` in large clusters) should have their cache size tuned via `--watch-cache-sizes=pods#1000,events#0`. Setting a resource's cache to 0 disables caching for that type and pushes all watch traffic directly to etcd — useful for resources that are rarely watched but have large objects.

The metric `watch_cache_resource_version` (label: `resource`) tracks the cache's current RV. A value significantly behind etcd's compaction watermark indicates the cache is not keeping up and clients will experience frequent relist events.

### 9.2 Performance: Admission Latency

Every mutating or creating request passes through the full admission chain synchronously. Slow webhooks directly increase P99 latency for all writes. The filter `WithAudit` annotates audit events with per-stage timestamps; the metric `apiserver_admission_webhook_request_total{name, operation, type, rejected}` and `apiserver_admission_webhook_latencies_seconds` track per-webhook performance.

To isolate the impact: a single slow webhook adds its full RTT to every matching request. Webhook timeout defaults are 10s; a webhook timing out results in a 500 (fail-open) or a rejection (fail-closed), depending on `failurePolicy`.

### 9.3 Reliability: Health Endpoints

The server exposes three health gates via `NonGoRestfulMux`:
- `/healthz` — liveness. Fails only on catastrophic internal state. Added checks via `AddHealthChecks`.
- `/readyz` — readiness. Fails during startup and the shutdown delay period (as soon as `ShutdownInitiated` fires). Custom storage readiness checks can be added via `StorageReadinessHook`.
- `/livez` — liveness with a grace period (`livezGracePeriod`) that allows the server to start up without being killed by kubelet.

`MuxAndDiscoveryComplete` is a lifecycle signal used by the `WithMuxAndDiscoveryComplete` filter to return `503` for requests that arrive before all paths are registered. This prevents 404 responses from being cached by clients during server startup.

### 9.4 Maintainability: Code Generation Dependencies

The `apiserver` library's build correctness depends on running several code generators before compilation:
- `deepcopy-gen`: for `DeepCopyObject()` on all registered types.
- `conversion-gen`: for conversion functions between internal and external API versions.
- `defaulter-gen`: for `SetDefaults_*` functions.
- `openapi-gen`: for `GetOpenAPIDefinitions()` used to build the `/openapi/v2` and `/openapi/v3` specs.

Forgetting to regenerate code after adding a new field is a common source of subtle bugs — the field will be silently dropped during conversion if no conversion function copies it, and will be missing from OpenAPI if not annotated.

### 9.5 Scalability: Resource Version Monotonicity

All `resourceVersion` values are etcd `mod_revision` values — globally monotonically increasing across all resources. This simplifies consistency reasoning (there is a total ordering of all writes) but means the RV of a `ConfigMap` and a `Pod` are comparable in magnitude. Clients that filter watches by a very high RV may cause the cacher's `waitUntilFreshAndBlock` to wait indefinitely if the RV is ahead of the cache — exposing a denial-of-service surface. The `WatchCacheReadWait` histogram tracks time spent in this blocking path.

---

## 10. Important Files and Directories

```
staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/
├── ARCHITECTURE.md                          # High-level design overview (canonical)
├── pkg/
│   ├── server/
│   │   ├── config.go                        # Config struct; DefaultBuildHandlerChain
│   │   ├── genericapiserver.go              # GenericAPIServer, DelegationTarget, PrepareRun, RunWithContext
│   │   ├── handler.go                       # APIServerHandler, Director (go-restful vs. mux routing)
│   │   ├── lifecycle_signals.go             # Named shutdown/startup signals
│   │   ├── options/
│   │   │   ├── recommended.go               # RecommendedOptions (entry point for extension servers)
│   │   │   └── etcd.go                      # EtcdOptions, watch cache sizing, encryption config
│   │   ├── filters/
│   │   │   ├── maxinflight.go               # Simple semaphore-based rate limiter
│   │   │   ├── timeout.go                   # WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests
│   │   │   └── waitgroup.go                 # WithWaitGroup (graceful drain)
│   │   └── healthz/                         # /healthz, /readyz, /livez implementations
│   │
│   ├── endpoints/
│   │   ├── installer.go                     # APIInstaller: route registration from rest.Storage
│   │   ├── filters/

│   │

│   │
